words.fact: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. led the Civil Rights Movement through nonviolent protest.
Rosa Parks sparked the Montgomery Bus Boycott by refusing to give up her seat.
Voting rights were a major focus of civil rights activism.
Marches helped activists gather support and demand justice.
Civil rights protect people from discrimination and unequal treatment.
Unity gave civil rights movements strength.
Many civil rights leaders promoted nonviolent change.
Justice means fairness under the law.
George Washington Carver improved agriculture with crop research.
Lewis Latimer improved the carbon filament used in light bulbs.
A patent protects an inventor's idea.
Black scientists have shaped many modern discoveries.
STEM stands for science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.
Latimer's work improved electric lighting.
Every invention begins with an idea.
Inventors build, test, and improve designs.
Maya Angelou was a poet and civil rights activist.
James Baldwin wrote influential essays and novels.
Ella Fitzgerald was known as the First Lady of Song.
Duke Ellington was a legendary jazz composer.
Jazz music grew from African American musical traditions.
Poetry expresses thoughts, culture, and history.
Music has long told stories of struggle and celebration.
Writing preserves stories and culture.
Jackie Robinson broke Major League Baseball's color barrier in 1947.
Track and field includes many historic Black athletes.
Sports fields have often been places where barriers were broken.
Great athletes help lead their teams.
Leadership matters on and off the field.
Goals represent determination and focus.
Sports history includes many important breakthroughs.
Winning can inspire future generations.
